title: Select synthesis providers per user without guessing subscription billing
context: Hunch supported Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor synthesis CLIs, but normal selection used registry order. On a machine with multiple installed CLIs that silently chose Claude first, even when the user could intend to use a different existing subscription. A committed setting would be equally unsafe because it could spend a teammate's plan. The no-direct-API rule remains essential, but must apply to every supported provider rather than one vendor.
decision: Resolve normal synthesis in this order: a valid HUNCH_SYNTH_PROVIDER shell override; a gitignored per-user `.hunch/local.json` preference managed by `hunch provider <name>`; automatic selection only when exactly one subscription CLI is available; otherwise use the deterministic local fallback. Never infer which of several installed subscriptions to bill. Claude, Codex, and Cursor implement the same SynthProvider contract; each provider strips any API credential that would override its subscription CLI auth. Deep synthesis remains explicit opt-in and may fan out to all available subscription CLIs.
consequences: `hunch provider` makes the active choice, availability, and safe fallback visible; `hunch doctor` reports why a provider was selected.
The local choice merges into `.hunch/local.json`, which is gitignored, so it preserves overlay settings and never becomes a team billing policy.
When several assistant CLIs are installed but no choice is made, normal capture, verification, auto-review, and wiki prose stay deterministic and free instead of silently spending a plan.
The blocking synthesis invariant is provider-agnostic: direct Anthropic/OpenAI SDK or endpoint use in synthesis remains forbidden, while the existing CLI-only execution paths remain allowed.
alternatives_rejected: Keep Claude-first registry order: it is a hidden product preference and can bill the wrong subscription.
Persist a provider in committed Hunch configuration: one developer's choice must not decide another developer's subscription usage.
Prompt the user interactively during every automatic hook: hooks must be non-blocking and unattended; deterministic fallback is safe and predictable.
Make a direct API provider configurable: it creates a separate billing path and violates the local subscription-only model.
